##NAMES=Pyroxferroite
##RRUFFID=R141080
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Fe^2+^SiO_3_
##LOCALITY=Caspar quarry, Bellerberg volcano, Ettringen, Mayen, Eifel, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Ch. and H. Schäfer
##DESCRIPTION=Red-brown isolated crystals, associated with colorless tridymite, sanidine, and clinoferrosilite (R141085). Crystal is twinned by 180 deg rotation around (111).
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R141080
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 6.608 b: 7.502 c: 17.359 alpha: 114.06 beta: 82.37 gamma: 94.36 volume: 778.6 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
